author: Ruben Asua
scope: Synthetic Microsoft Entra sign-in and audit logs only.

detections.title: Repeated Failed Sign-ins From One IP
detections.threshold: 5 failed sign-ins in 5 minutes per IP and app
detections.severity_reason: A noisy failed source can indicate password guessing, but source ownership and expected testing must be reviewed before escalation.
detections.tuning: Baseline identity test systems, VPN concentrators and known managed service traffic before excluding.

narrative.what_happened: Several failed sign-ins were observed from one source IP against the same cloud application.
narrative.why_it_matters: Concentrated failures can be an early indicator of password guessing or a broken automation path.
narrative.what_to_check_next: Confirm whether the IP belongs to expected infrastructure, review affected users and compare with recent support or testing activity.
narrative.decision_example: Keep the alert when the source is not owned or when failures continue across a short window.

detections.title: Failed Sign-ins Across Multiple Users
detections.threshold: 5 distinct users with failures in 10 minutes per IP and app
detections.severity_reason: One source touching many accounts raises password spraying concern and deserves faster review than single-user failure bursts.
detections.tuning: Document trusted gateways and scripted identity validation sources with owner and expiration.

narrative.what_happened: One source generated failed sign-ins across several fictional users in a short window.
narrative.why_it_matters: Many accounts from one source is a stronger signal for password spraying than repeated attempts on one user.
narrative.what_to_check_next: Check user count, source ownership, affected applications and whether Conditional Access or risk controls intervened.
narrative.decision_example: Escalate when the source is unfamiliar or when failures span privileged or sensitive users.

detections.title: MFA Denied Repeatedly Followed By Success
detections.threshold: 2 MFA denied failures followed by success in 15 minutes for the same user and IP
detections.severity_reason: Repeated MFA denial followed by success is a high-context sequence that can justify urgent account verification.
detections.tuning: Compare with known user travel, device enrollment and helpdesk-assisted recovery before creating exceptions.

narrative.what_happened: The same user denied MFA more than once and later produced a successful sign-in from the same source.
narrative.why_it_matters: The sequence can represent user fatigue, mistaken approval or a legitimate recovery flow that still needs validation.
narrative.what_to_check_next: Contact the user through an approved channel, inspect device and app context, and review recent MFA registration changes.
narrative.decision_example: Treat as priority review when the success happens quickly after repeated denials.

detections.title: High-risk Sign-in Not Blocked
detections.threshold: Successful medium or high risk sign-in where Conditional Access did not fail
detections.severity_reason: Successful risky sign-ins should be reviewed because policy, licensing and response gaps can leave access active.
detections.tuning: Risk fields are synthetic here and may be hidden or unavailable in some real tenants depending on licensing and retention.

narrative.what_happened: A synthetic sign-in with elevated risk succeeded without a Conditional Access failure result.
narrative.why_it_matters: Risk without blocking is a control review signal, not proof of compromise, but it can reveal policy gaps.
narrative.what_to_check_next: Review Conditional Access result, risk state, user risk history, session controls and whether Identity Protection data is available.
narrative.decision_example: Escalate if the account is sensitive or the risk event aligns with unfamiliar location, app or device context.

detections.title: Privileged Role Assignment
detections.threshold: Successful assignment to Global Administrator or Privileged Role Administrator
detections.severity_reason: Privileged role assignment changes identity blast radius and should be reviewed against approved change records.
detections.tuning: Tune only with change ticket, approver, role, assignee and time-bounded approval context.

narrative.what_happened: A privileged directory role was assigned successfully in synthetic audit data.
narrative.why_it_matters: Role assignment can immediately expand administrative capability and persistence options.
narrative.what_to_check_next: Validate the initiator, target identity, role, approval path and any follow-on administrative activity.
narrative.decision_example: Escalate when the assignment is outside an approved maintenance or access-review workflow.

detections.title: Application Credential Added
detections.threshold: Successful application or service principal credential update
detections.severity_reason: New app credentials can be legitimate rotation or a persistence concern depending on owner, app scope and timing.
detections.tuning: Baseline approved credential rotation pipelines and require owner, app id, expiration and change record.

narrative.what_happened: A credential-related property changed on a synthetic application or service principal object.
narrative.why_it_matters: Application credentials can allow non-interactive access and deserve clear ownership and expiry validation.
narrative.what_to_check_next: Review app owner, credential type, expiration, permissions and whether the change matches an approved rotation.
narrative.decision_example: Keep the alert when credential changes happen outside expected automation or involve sensitive app permissions.
